title: Foundation models are becoming multimodal, tool-using and agentic
description: Frontier AI evidence supports a shift from chatbot-like systems toward systems with multimodal inputs/outputs, tools, memory and more agent-like operation.
downstream_use: Authorised for Stage 4 technical condition modelling only.
prohibited_overextension: Do not infer AGI or reliable autonomy.

title: Inference-time compute and scaffolding are capability levers
description: Search, verification, tool use, budget forcing and other test-time methods can improve some reasoning/coding outcomes with cost and latency tradeoffs.
downstream_use: Authorised for Stage 4 technical condition modelling only.
prohibited_overextension: Do not treat thinking longer as universally better.

title: Coding agents and AI R&D automation are improving in bounded evaluable settings
description: Software and research-engineering tasks show meaningful progress, especially where correctness can be tested.
downstream_use: Authorised for Stage 4 technical condition modelling only.
prohibited_overextension: Do not claim replacement of engineers or open-ended research autonomy.

title: Recursive self-improvement is not established
description: AlphaEvolve and AI Scientist-like systems show bounded loops under fixed models, scaffolds and evaluators, not general open-ended recursive self-improvement.
downstream_use: Authorised for Stage 4 technical condition modelling only.
prohibited_overextension: Do not present recursive self-improvement as demonstrated.

title: Generative software and GUI agents may make software more dynamic
description: GUI agents and natural-language software generation can operate and create digital workflows, but verification, security and maintainability remain central bottlenecks.
downstream_use: Authorised for Stage 4 technical condition modelling only.
prohibited_overextension: Do not claim software complexity disappears.

title: Robotics foundation models are progressing under bounded conditions
description: Cross-embodiment datasets, VLA models and generalist policies show progress toward flexible robot control.
downstream_use: Authorised for Stage 4 technical condition modelling only.
prohibited_overextension: Do not infer general-purpose robots from lab results.

title: Robotics deployment remains physically bottlenecked
description: Industrial robots are deployed at scale, but general embodied AI is constrained by dexterity, data, safety, integration cost, uptime and long-tail environments.
downstream_use: Authorised for Stage 4 technical condition modelling only.
prohibited_overextension: Do not assume robotics follows software timelines.

title: AI is materially useful in selected science domains
description: Protein structure, protein design and materials screening show concrete AI gains where data and validation targets are comparatively strong.
downstream_use: Authorised for Stage 4 technical condition modelling only.
prohibited_overextension: Do not claim AI has solved science.

title: Discovery acceleration is not validated real-world impact
description: Scientific, medical and engineering AI can accelerate substeps, but experiments, trials, toxicity, manufacturing, regulation and deployment remain bottlenecks.
downstream_use: Authorised for Stage 4 technical condition modelling only.
prohibited_overextension: Do not treat generated candidates as therapies/products.

title: Automated labs are promising but narrow and correction-prone
description: Automated prediction-synthesis-characterisation loops can accelerate selected workflows but remain narrow and need independent reproduction.
downstream_use: Authorised for Stage 4 technical condition modelling only.
prohibited_overextension: Do not claim automated labs can conduct open-ended science.

title: AI is infrastructure-constrained
description: Compute, chips, memory, networking, data centres, electricity, water and capital shape AI deployment.
downstream_use: Authorised for Stage 4 technical condition modelling only.
prohibited_overextension: Do not treat compute growth as destiny or constraints as fatal.

title: Open-weight and edge AI may diversify capability
description: Open foundation models and edge intelligence can decentralise access and control while complicating monitoring and governance.
downstream_use: Authorised for Stage 4 technical condition modelling only.
prohibited_overextension: Do not treat open AI as simply safe or unsafe.

title: Sovereign AI may fragment technical futures
description: National clouds, local-language models, export controls and domestic compute programmes may create divergent AI ecosystems.
downstream_use: Authorised for Stage 4 technical condition modelling only.
prohibited_overextension: Do not assume one global AI stack.

title: Cyber and bio misuse risks require operational uplift evidence
description: Current evidence supports productivity/scaling uplift more strongly than widespread novel autonomous cyber or bio capability.
downstream_use: Authorised for Stage 4 technical condition modelling only.
prohibited_overextension: Do not turn concern into demonstrated catastrophe.

title: Synthetic media is a real trust condition but effects vary
description: Deepfakes and synthetic media are present in scams, harassment and politics, but reach and persuasion vary and detection is fragile.
downstream_use: Authorised for Stage 4 technical condition modelling only.
prohibited_overextension: Do not assume trust collapse.

title: AI companions and social agents are distinct adjacent trajectories
description: Companion systems involve repeated relational interaction, memory and disclosure, creating distinct opportunities and risks.
downstream_use: Authorised for Stage 4 technical condition modelling only.
prohibited_overextension: Do not equate all companion use with harm.

title: Surveillance, sensing and neurotechnology require cautious tracking
description: AI-enabled sensing and biometrics are active conditions; neurotechnology warrants watchlist status for privacy and neural-data governance.
downstream_use: Authorised for Stage 4 technical condition modelling only.
prohibited_overextension: Do not present neurotechnology as mind-reading or mass cognitive control.

title: Conservative tool acceleration
description: AI improves tools but not reliable unsupervised long-horizon autonomy.
downstream_use: Stage 4 may use as technical condition band.
prohibited_overextension: Do not treat as prediction.

title: Agentic software acceleration

prohibited_overextension: Do not treat as prediction.

title: Specialist talk completed: Agents, Coding and Self-Improvement
description: Talk central mechanism: AI becomes action when models are coupled to evaluable environments; coding and AI R&D progress is strongest where outputs can be run, tested or scored; recursive self-improvement remains unresolved.
relevance: Durable logging for reopened Stage 3 specialist synthesis.
downstream_use: Feeds cross-agent discussion and editorial synthesis only.
prohibited_overextension: Do not treat specialist talk as final stage conclusion before discussion and audit.
notes: Pass-forward candidates: bounded/evaluable coding-agent acceleration; inference-time compute as lever; benchmark caution; AI R&D acceleration only where programmatic evaluation exists.

title: Specialist talk completed: Generative Software and Interfaces
description: Talk central mechanism: software may move from fixed interface to negotiated execution, where GUI agents and generated workflows operate existing software but make verification, security and accountability central.
relevance: Durable logging for reopened Stage 3 specialist synthesis.
downstream_use: Feeds cross-agent discussion and editorial synthesis only.
prohibited_overextension: Do not treat specialist talk as final stage conclusion before discussion and audit.
notes: Pass-forward candidates: GUI agents as credible pathway; software-agent evaluation fragile; dynamic/agent-operated workflows plausible but not inevitable.

title: Specialist talk completed: Robotics and Embodied AI
description: Talk central mechanism: robotics is beginning to inherit foundation-model generalisation but cannot escape physical-world constraints; demo-versus-deployment distinction is central.
relevance: Durable logging for reopened Stage 3 specialist synthesis.
downstream_use: Feeds cross-agent discussion and editorial synthesis only.
prohibited_overextension: Do not treat specialist talk as final stage conclusion before discussion and audit.
notes: Pass-forward candidates: industrial robots deployed at scale in structured settings; robot foundation models progressing under bounded conditions; service/domestic deployment constrained by safety, data, dexterity, cost and environment variation.

title: Specialist talk completed: AI for Science, Medicine and Engineering
description: Talk central mechanism: AI turns parts of discovery into search and generation, but candidate generation is not validated impact; validation is the spinal distinction.
relevance: Durable logging for reopened Stage 3 specialist synthesis.
downstream_use: Feeds cross-agent discussion and editorial synthesis only.
prohibited_overextension: Do not treat specialist talk as final stage conclusion before discussion and audit.
notes: Pass-forward candidates: AI useful in selected scientific subdomains; discovery acceleration separate from clinical/product impact; automated labs promising but narrow and correction-prone.

relevance: Durable logging for reopened Stage 3 specialist synthesis.
downstream_use: Feeds cross-agent discussion and editorial synthesis only.
prohibited_overextension: Do not treat specialist talk as final stage conclusion before discussion and audit.
notes: Pass-forward candidates: foundation models as multimodal/tool-using systems; inference-time compute as lever; long-task evaluation as signpost; long context is not memory; model-plus-scaffold framing.

title: Specialist talk completed: Infrastructure, Compute, Open and Edge AI

title: Chaired cross-agent discussion: emergent synthesis
description: Cross-agent discussion identified the strongest emergent mechanism: AI advances fastest where capability can be embedded in feedback-rich, evaluable systems and slows where verification, embodiment, infrastructure, law, trust and human accountability make reality expensive.
relevance: Authorises the editor to adopt an emergent thesis after specialist talks rather than before them.
scope: Existing source rows and logged specialist talks only.
limitations: Feedback-loop mechanism must not be treated as the only causal engine; adoption, power, governance, cost and infrastructure remain co-determinants.
counterevidence: Red team warned that a neat sequence could smuggle in inevitability and underplay adoption/governance/power.
uncertainty_description: How strongly feedback-rich acceleration generalises outside software-like domains remains uncertain.
downstream_use: Frames final Stage 3 report and technical condition bands for Stage 4.
prohibited_overextension: Do not treat evaluable loops as a universal law or predicted sequence of technological arrival.
revision_trigger: Reopen if strong evidence shows broad real-world autonomy without dense feedback or if adoption conditions dominate technical capability in later analysis.
notes: Recommended report backbone: the price of reality-checking.

title: Adversarial red team: weaken elegant acceleration narrative
description: Red team accepted the feedback/verification thesis as useful but required that it remain one accelerator among model capacity, tools, infrastructure, permissions, adoption environments, governance and market power.
relevance: Prevents narrative seduction and hype in final report.
scope: Existing Stage 3 source rows and logged talks only.
limitations: Red team is a reasoning audit, not new empirical evidence.
bias_or_access_issue: Narrative structure may overweight frontier AI and benchmark evidence if not caveated.
ethical_issue: Public misunderstanding risk if report implies inevitability.
downstream_use: Defines required weakenings for report, review and pass-forward.
prohibited_overextension: No established recursive self-improvement; no coding replacement claim; no robotics software-timeline transfer; no governance-as-safety; no truth-collapse rhetoric.
revision_trigger: Reopen if final report uses any rejected overclaim.
notes: Recommended gate status: pass_with_cautions after rewrite.

title: Editorial decision: adopt emergent thesis and explanatory sequence
description: After specialist talks, chaired discussion, evidence/bias audit and red-team critique, the editor adopted the emergent thesis that AI futures depend on the price of converting capability into verified action, not on a single intelligence curve.
relevance: Documents that the final thesis and chapter sequence were decided after the discussion phase.
scope: Final report structure only; does not authorise Stage 4 world-building or curriculum design.
limitations: The sequence is explanatory for readers, not a prediction of arrival order.
counterevidence: Red team warned against treating feedback loops as a master key; report must retain infrastructure, adoption, governance and power constraints.
bias_or_access_issue: High-resource and frontier-lab source bias remains visible.
downstream_use: Structures STAGE_REPORT.md and filters PASS_FORWARD.md.
prohibited_overextension: Do not present the chapter order as a forecast, roadmap or inevitability.
revision_trigger: Reopen if final review finds pre-baked structure, unsupported source use or Stage 4/curriculum drift.
notes: Central phrase: where reality answers cheaply, AI can accelerate; where reality is embodied, regulated, adversarial or humanly meaningful, progress becomes slower and more institution-dependent.
